title: [setup] Scaffold the Rust workspace and contributor rules
body:
**Category:** setup
**Automation:** auto
**Depends on:** none
**Produces:** `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `AGENTS.md`, `crates/moria-world/`, `crates/moria-demo/`, `crates/moria-bench/`, `crates/moria-curate/`

## Description

Create the deliberate four-package Rust 2024/Bevy 0.19 workspace and thin package entry points described by the TDD, including the repository-root contributor rules. Reference: docs/tdd/overview.md.

## Inputs

- `docs/tdd/overview.md` — authoritative contract and acceptance details

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The four packages build with the pinned direct dependencies and committed lockfile
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` reproduces the commands, naming, schedule, public-boundary, portability, and feasibility-gate rules
- [ ] Dev and native release profiles match the TDD and assets remain at repository root
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass

title: [setup] Add deterministic headless test infrastructure
body:
**Category:** setup
**Automation:** auto
**Depends on:** M-001
**Produces:** `crates/moria-world/src/testing.rs`, `crates/moria-world/tests/`, `crates/moria-demo/tests/`

## Description

Provide the controlled fixed-tick and headless-app test foundation used by every later system without wall-clock sleeps or real windows. Reference: docs/tdd/overview.md.

## Inputs

- `M-001` — completed prerequisite contract and artifacts
- `docs/tdd/overview.md` — authoritative contract and acceptance details

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `run_fixed_ticks` executes and verifies an exact requested fixed-tick count
- [ ] MinimalPlugins fixtures seed only required resources, messages, states, and entities
- [ ] An external-consumer fixture proves tests use the public `moria-world` facade
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass

title: [setup] Define shared asset IDs and loader declarations
body:
**Category:** setup
**Automation:** auto
**Depends on:** M-001, M-002
**Produces:** `crates/moria-world/src/presentation/asset_ids.rs`, `crates/moria-world/src/presentation/assets.rs`

## Description

Predeclare the complete immutable table of all 30 runtime asset stable IDs and final paths, plus shared loader, fallback-warning, and validation infrastructure; later per-file scaffolds consume this table without editing it. Reference: docs/tdd/assets.md.

## Inputs

- `M-001` — completed prerequisite contract and artifacts
- `M-002` — completed prerequisite contract and artifacts
- `docs/tdd/assets.md` — authoritative contract and acceptance details

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The declaration table contains every inventory path in assets.md exactly once with an immutable stable ID and no per-asset follow-up mutation required
- [ ] Loader declarations distinguish required, development-fallback, and release-fatal behavior and expose a unique validation-fixture hook per ID
- [ ] Unit tests reject missing/duplicate IDs, duplicate paths, undeclared runtime loads, and any table cardinality other than 30
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass

- `docs/tdd/assets.md; docs/tdd/data-model.md` — authoritative generated-manifest and digest contracts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The placeholder satisfies canonical generated RON with generated-file comment, sorted features/water/objects/route, and a `parameters_digest` computed from the exact M-008 and M-009 bytes
- [ ] Its isolated asset-schema test resolves the exact predeclared ID/path, makes fallback use observable, and proves changing either digest source invalidates the manifest
- [ ] The issue does not edit shared declaration or registry files, and the stable ID/path and shared-handle role remain immutable for acquisition and wire-in
- [ ] Tests exist and pass
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests still pass
